title: A meal that took longer than I thought
summary: Narrate the experience of cooking a meal that took much longer than you expected.
topic: A meal that took longer than I thought
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a time you cooked a meal that took much longer than you expected. Describe what you were making and what went wrong or took extra time. Use past simple and past continuous. Say how the meal turned out in the end.
hintStarters: I decided to cook … for the first time, thinking it would only take about…
Everything was going well at first, but then…
By the time the meal was finally ready, it was already…
sampleText: Last Saturday, I decided to make a lamb stew for the first time in my small apartment in Edinburgh. I found a recipe online and it said the dish would take about one hour. I went to the market that morning and bought everything I needed. When I got home, I started cutting the vegetables and browning the meat. Everything was going well at first. But after thirty minutes, I noticed the sauce was much thinner than the photo in the recipe. I turned down the heat and let it simmer for another thirty minutes, but it still did not look right. I added more tomatoes and some extra herbs and stirred the pot every few minutes. The whole kitchen smelled wonderful, but the stew was not ready. Two hours passed and it was already eight o'clock at night. I was starving and a little frustrated. Finally, after two and a half hours, the sauce was thick and the meat was soft and tender. I sat down at the table and took the first bite. It tasted much better than I expected. My flatmate came home while I was eating and asked for a bowl. She said it was the best stew she had ever tried. I was proud, but I decided that next time I would start cooking much earlier.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Everything was going well at first

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngon hơn nhiều so với tôi mong đợi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: next time I would start cooking much earlier

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous interrupted by past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous cho hành động đang xảy ra khi bị gián đoạn bởi một hành động khác (past simple): 'My flatmate came home while I was eating.' Cấu trúc: while + past continuous, past simple.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous for what was in progress when another action interrupted it: 'My flatmate came home while I was eating.' Pattern: while + past continuous, past simple.

sampleTranslationVi: Thứ Bảy tuần trước, tôi quyết định làm món hầm thịt cừu lần đầu tiên trong căn hộ nhỏ của mình ở Edinburgh. Tôi tìm thấy một công thức trên mạng và nó nói rằng món ăn sẽ mất khoảng một giờ. Tôi đi chợ sáng hôm đó và mua tất cả những gì cần thiết. Khi về nhà, tôi bắt đầu cắt rau và áp chảo thịt. Mọi thứ ban đầu đều đang diễn ra tốt. Nhưng sau ba mươi phút, tôi nhận thấy nước sốt loãng hơn nhiều so với ảnh trong công thức. Tôi vặn nhỏ lửa và để nó đun liu riu thêm ba mươi phút, nhưng vẫn trông chưa đúng. Tôi thêm cà chua và một ít thảo mộc rồi khuấy nồi vài phút một lần. Cả nhà bếp thơm tuyệt vời, nhưng món hầm chưa sẵn sàng. Hai giờ trôi qua và đã là tám giờ tối. Tôi đang rất đói và hơi bực bội. Cuối cùng, sau hai tiếng rưỡi, nước sốt đã đặc và thịt mềm. Tôi ngồi xuống bàn và ăn miếng đầu tiên. Nó ngon hơn nhiều so với tôi mong đợi. Bạn cùng phòng về nhà khi tôi đang ăn và xin một bát. Cô ấy nói đó là món hầm ngon nhất cô từng thử. Tôi tự hào, nhưng quyết định lần sau sẽ bắt đầu nấu sớm hơn nhiều.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did the recipe say the dish would take?
mcQuestions.explanation: The recipe said it would take about one hour, but it actually took two and a half hours.

mcQuestions.prompt: What was the first problem the writer noticed?
options: The sauce was much thinner than the photo in the recipe
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer noticed the sauce was much thinner than expected.
mcQuestions.hint: What did the writer see after thirty minutes?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'while + past continuous'?
options: My flatmate came home while I ate.
My flatmate was come home while I was eating.
My flatmate came home while eating I was.
My flatmate came home while I was eating.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While' + past continuous describes what was happening when the main event occurred.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct phrasal verb: 'I ___ the heat and let it simmer.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Turn down' means to reduce the level of something, like heat or volume.
mcQuestions.hint: To reduce the heat, you 'turn it ___'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'turned out' mean in 'the meal turned out well'?
options: The meal was removed from the pot
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Turn out' means to have a particular result or end in a certain way.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Turned out well' means the final result was...

mcQuestions.prompt: How long did the stew actually take?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'finally, after two and a half hours, the sauce was thick.'
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about when the stew was finally ready.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means the meat was soft and pleasant to eat?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tender' describes meat that is soft and easy to chew.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the writer add to improve the sauce?
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer added more tomatoes and some extra herbs.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence about what the writer added.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparison: 'It tasted ___ better than I expected.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Much better' is the correct comparative form to emphasise the degree of difference.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word strengthens a comparative adjective?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means you used everything and had none left?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Ran out of' is the natural phrasal verb for having none of something left.
mcQuestions.hint: The phrasal verb 'run out of' means you have no more of something.

mcQuestions.prompt: What lesson did the writer learn?
options: To ask a friend for help
To start cooking much earlier next time
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says: 'I decided that next time I would start cooking much earlier.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever cooked something that took much longer than you planned? What is the most important lesson you can learn from cooking a difficult meal for the first time?
